solution: ```class FreqStack:

    def __init__(self):
        self.freq = {}
        self.group = {}
        self.maxf = 0

    def push(self, val: int) -> None:
        f = self.freq.get(val, 0) + 1
        self.freq[val] = f
        if f > self.maxf:
            self.maxf = f
        self.group.setdefault(f, []).append(val)

    def pop(self) -> int:
        val = self.group[self.maxf].pop()
        self.freq[val] -= 1
        if not self.group[self.maxf]:
            self.maxf -= 1
        return val


# Your FreqStack object will be instantiated and called as such:
# obj = FreqStack()
# obj.push(val)
# param_2 = obj.pop()```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses two dictionaries: one to track per-value frequencies and another to store stacks of values keyed by frequency. push updates both in O(1); pop removes from the highest-frequency stack in O(1). Space is proportional to total pushes.
